channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: Here are our Xbox Game Pass games for June
item.content:encoded:
Herdling! Persona 5 Royal! Frog Sqwad! More!

Microsoft has revealed the first batch of games coming to its Xbox Game Pass subscription service this June.

Game Pass subscribers can look forward to the following games making their way onto the service in the coming weeks:

  • Herdling (Cloud, Xbox Series X/S, and PC) – 4th June (today), now with Game Pass Premium; joining Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass
  • Total Chaos (Cloud, Xbox Series X/S, and PC) – 4th June (today), now with Game Pass Premium; joining Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass
  • Solarpunk (Cloud, Xbox Series X/S, and PC) – 8th June, via Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass. Available day one with Xbox Game Pass.
  • Undisputed (Cloud, Xbox Series X/S, Handheld, and PC) – 8th June via Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • Persona 5 Royal (Cloud, Console, and PC) – 9th June, via Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • Beastro (Cloud, Xbox Series X/S, Handheld, and PC) – 11th June via Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Frog Sqwad (Cloud, Xbox Series X/S, and PC) – 11th June via Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Starseeker: Astroneer Expeditions (Game Preview) (Cloud, XBOX Series X/S, and PC) – 11th June via Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass. Available day one on Xbox Game Pass
  • Junkster (Cloud, Xbox Series X/S, Handheld, and PC) – 16th June via Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass. Available day one on Xbox Game Pass

So, what of those games? Personally, I am keen to give Herdling a go. This game is described as a "grand alpine expedition" which sees you ascending a mountain path with a herd of loveable beasts accompanying you. We awarded Herdling four stars on its release last year, with Jim Trinca calling it "a powerful, memorable story told not with dialogue but with interaction, movement, and art" in Eurogamer's review.

I will also give Frog Sqwad - a co-op extraction puzzle-platformer - a go with my kids. I hadn't heard of this one before, but the official blurb says you will "storm the sewers for the glory of the Swamp King", which sounds like something my son will enjoy.

As for turn-based RPG Persona 5 Royal, we called it "both better and worse" than the original. "Great new gameplay features can't help the fact that Persona 5 Royal drags on even more than its predecessor," Malindy Hetfeld wrote in Eurogamer's review.

As with every month, with new games coming to the Game Pass service, it does mean a selection of games will be leaving the library. On 15th June, the following with be leaving the service:

  • Jurassic World Evolution 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Lost in Random: The Eternal Die (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Scott Pilgrim vs. The World (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Warhammer 40K Space Marine: Master Crafted Edition (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Shredder’s Revenge (Cloud, Console, and PC)

For everything else in Microsoft's subscription service, you can check out our handy Xbox Game Pass guide detailing the many titles available.

Mentioned in this article

Herdling

Nintendo Switch, PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S
